questions.question: What are Known Answer Tests (KAT)?
questions.explanation: KAT vectors verify correct implementation against known outputs.

questions.question: What latency increase is acceptable for hybrid TLS?
questions.explanation: Typically 10-50% increase is acceptable, depending on application.

questions.question: What tests verify constant-time implementation?
questions.explanation: Tools like ctgrind detect timing variations in cryptographic code.

questions.question: What must hybrid KEM tests verify?
questions.explanation: Both components must work and combine properly.

questions.question: What P-value latency is typically monitored?
questions.explanation: P50 (median), P95, and P99 capture typical and tail latencies.

questions.question: Who signs off on go-live for PQC migration?

options.text: Multiple roles (engineering, security, QA, ops, business)

questions.explanation: Multiple stakeholders verify different aspects before go-live.

questions.question: Security testing should include algorithm downgrade attack testing.
questions.explanation: Downgrade attacks attempt to force weaker algorithms.
